title: Co-Bidding Patterns in Federal Procurement
subtitle: When the same vendors repeatedly appear in the same competitive tenders — and take turns winning — statistical patterns emerge that warrant closer examination.

stats.totalPairsSub: unique vendor pairs tracked across all sectors
stats.flaggedPairsSub: co-bid rate above 50% with 10+ shared procedures
stats.sharedProceduresSub: total co-bidding events among flagged pairs

methodology.title: How RUBLI identifies co-bidding patterns
methodology.body: We analyze every competitive tender in COMPRANET to find vendor pairs that bid on the same procedures at abnormally high rates. A co-bid rate above 50% with 10+ shared procedures is a statistical anomaly that may warrant further investigation — particularly when combined with low win-rate variance or one vendor consistently not winning. These patterns are invisible in individual contracts but emerge clearly at scale. High co-bid rates alone are not proof of coordination — they are a signal for investigative follow-up.

patterns.title: Co-Bidding Patterns
patterns.bidRotationDesc: Pattern consistent with bid rotation: vendors alternate wins — A wins round 1, B wins round 2. Win rates are nearly equal.
patterns.coverBiddingDesc: Pattern consistent with cover bidding: one vendor consistently does not win, while the other wins repeatedly in shared procedures.
patterns.marketAllocationDesc: Pattern consistent with market allocation: vendors appear in different institution or geographic segments without competing against each other.
patterns.unknownDesc: Pattern detected but not yet classified — requires further investigation.

tooltip.coBidRateBody: Co-bid rate is the percentage of procedures where both vendors submitted bids simultaneously. Rates above 70% combined with high shared procedure volume are a statistical anomaly that may indicate coordinated bidding — a pattern that warrants investigative follow-up but is not conclusive evidence of coordination.

empty.title: No pairs match current filters
empty.body: Try lowering the minimum shared procedures threshold or disabling the flagged-only filter. Co-bidding pairs with fewer than 10 shared procedures are excluded by default because isolated co-bidding is normal market behavior.

error.title: COMPRANET co-bidding data unavailable
error.body: The collusion analysis endpoint returned an error. This may indicate the co_bidding_stats table needs to be rebuilt. Try again or check backend logs.
